Section 2105 provides that a 
reign

Section 171 provides 
a foreign corpora-
tion does not include any domestic corporation or for Chapter 21 
any corporation chartered under the laws of the United States. 
Section 167 defines a domestic corporation as any corporation formed 
under California law. 
Section 2105 also does not apply to any 
proprietorship or partnership. 
Section 191 defines transacting intra state business to mean entering 
into repeated and successive transactions of its business in this 
state other than interstate or foreign commerce. 
The reference to 
interstate or foreign commerce means eng 
ing exclusively in inter-
state or foreign commerce in the constitutional sense. 
Section 191 
provides that a parent is not deemed to be doing business in the 
state merely because its subsidiary is doing business in the state.

sections.question_synthetic: The document discusses California corporate registration requirements for foreign corporations doing intrastate business, including exemptions and the applicability of local business license ordinances.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is internal memorandum rather than formal advice letter response. Significant OCR degradation throughout (missing/corrupted characters). No formal Q/A structure. Contains preliminary research findings and discussion of unresolved issues rather than definitive FPPC advice. Low confidence due to text quality and format inconsistency with typical FPPC advice letters.

embedding.summary: This is an internal FPPC memorandum from Ted to Mike dated September 15, 1977, presenting preliminary research on California Corporations Code requirements for foreign corporations conducting intrastate business, including registration requirements, exemptions, and questions about local business license ordinances.
